rationale.R1.2: The paragraph contains a prominent inferential leap: a CTR improvement (24.1% vs 18.7%) is labeled 'direct evidence that CalmCPO reduces user interruption cost.' CTR measures engagement, not interruption cost; the logical bridge is missing, making consistency checking central to any substantive review of this text.
rationale.R1.3: The headline empirical claim — that deferring notifications reduces cognitive load — is not supported by the reported metric. CTR is a proxy for engagement quality, not an established measure of interruption cost or cognitive load. Evidence-to-claim alignment is materially at risk here.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on the scientific validity of the claims (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment), or on writing quality and presentation — or cover both equally?
